NavigateSLO Resource Guide
NavigateSLO Resource Guide
NavigateSLO (NavSLO) is designed to connect parents, children, teens, providers, and everyone in between with local services. Each section contains links and ways to contact local service providers and resources. Some links are to other community resource guides managed by other organizations.
These tips for using the NavigateSLO Resource Guide
will help you get started with the guide.
The Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health Program maintains NavSLO.
About NavSLO
This resource guide is:
designed for parents, providers, case workers, and community members in San Luis Obispo County;
updated and verified routinely by Public Health staff;
not an exhaustive list of every resource available, but these are commonly used;
not an endorsement of any service or organization by the Public Health Department;
informational.
Prefer a hard copy?
Check out the printer-friendly version of NavSLO
available in English and Spanish.
NavSLO- Digital
▾

Crisis & Emergency Hotlines
Emergency Law or Ambulance
Police, ambulance. Call if there is an active threat or emergency.
English, Spanish
Call or Text: 911
California Poison Control System Hotline
Poison-related help, questions, and advice
English and translation services available for over 200 other languages
Call: 1-800-222-1222
California Youth Crisis Line
Free, 24/7 emergency response and crisis support line for youth (12-24) and families
English, Spanish, and interpretation available
Call or Text: 1-800-843-5200
Central Coast Alcoholics Anonymous
Free, confidential, 24/7 hotline staffed by volunteers for those with problems with alcohol or who want to stop drinking
English
Call: 805-541-3211
County of SLO Adult Protective Services (APS)
Emergency intervention for dependent adults and seniors; Investigates allegations of abuse and intervenes when necessary
English, Spanish, and interpretation available
Call: 805-781-1790 M-F, 8am-5pm |Call: 805-729-8011 after business hours
County of SLO Child Welfare Services Emergency Reporting Line
24/7 hotline to file a Suspected Child Abuse Report (SCAR) in SLO County
English, Spanish, and interpretation available
Call: 805-781-5437 OR 805-781-1700

English, Spanish, ASL, and interpretation available

Free, confidential 24/7 hotline for survivors of domestic abuse and their families
English, Spanish, and other translation available
Call: 1-800-799-7233 | Text "Start" to" 88788
National Maternal Mental Health Hotline
Free, confidential, 24/7 hotline for pregnant and postpartum women and their families
English, Spanish, and other translation available
Call or Text: 833-TLC-MAMA (833-852-6262)

Free, confidential, 24/7 hotline for victims of human trafficking

Call: 1-888-373-7888 | Text: 233-733

Free, confidential, 24/7 crisis hotline for runaway youth or those at risk
English, Spanish
Call or Text: 1-800-RUNAWAY (1-800-786-2929) |
Chat online
SLO Hotline (Central Coast Hotline)
Free, confidential support for those struggling with mental health, postpartum depression, and suicidal thoughts
English, Spanish
Call or Text: 1-800-783-0607
Suicide and Crisis Lifeline

English, Spanish, and interpretation services available
Call or Text: 988 |
Chat online

Organization

Service Area

Basic Needs: Food & Nutrition & Clothing
40 Prado Homeless Services Center
Provides shelter, food, laundry, shower, mail services, pet kennels, and medical care for homeless individuals or those at risk
On site at 40 Prado Road, San Luis Obispo
Complete their referral form
| Call: 805-544-4004
ALF Food Pantry
Food pantry open Monday-Friday, 1-3PM | 5411 El Camino Real, Atascadero
Serving Atascadero, Templeton, Santa Margarita, Creston, and the California Valley
Call: 805-461-1504
Bento
Free, healthy groceries ordered via text message and delivered to your house; Available for certain CenCal & Medical patients
Serving California communities, including SLO County
Call: 1-800-936-3022 |
Email Bento
|
Apply online
CalFresh Food Assistance
Helps individuals and families purchase food with an EBT card and monthly stipend to use at grocery stores and some farmers' markets
Visit any
Social Services Office
to apply in person |
Apply online
Call: 805-781-1600
County of SLO Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
Food assistance using E-WIC debit cards, nutrition counseling, breastfeeding support, and referrals for eligible individuals;
Check your eligibility
Find a WIC office closest to you, plus pop-up events
Call: 805-781-5571 | Text: 888-417-6180
Goodwill Central Coast
Low-cost household goods and clothing; Can provide assistance with food and housing
Locations
in San Luis Obispo, Grover Beach, Atascadero, and Paso Robles

The Link Family Resource Center
Free family support services including food, housing, clothing, healthcare, and parent education;
Must be referred
Locations in Atascadero and Paso Robles
Call: 805-794-0217 |
Email Carrie
|
Refer a Family
Los Osos Cares/Womenade
Supports families with community meals, clothing, financial and housing assistance, and provides resources
Supporting the North Coast; Los Osos, Morro Bay, and Cayucos
Email Los Osos Cares
|
View their Programs
North County NeighborAid
Provides "gifts" to help families in crisis; "Gifts" can include gas cards, utility payments, food, rent, and more;
Must be referred by a human service worker or other professional
(teacher, clergy, etc)
Supports clients in North San Luis Obispo County
Email the team
|
Refer a Family
Pregnancy & Parenting Support of SLO County
Provides maternity & baby clothing, diapers, wipes, formula & baby food, nursing supplies, baby equipment, and new car seats; Services by appointment only
3480 S. Higuera St, Suite 100, San Luis Obispo
Call: 805-541-3367 to schedule |
Email PPS SLO
The Salvation Army
Provides emergency financial assistance for housing, food, utility bills, transportation, and more; Financial assistance by appointment only
Locations throughout SLO County
Call: 805-544-2401
South County Womenade
Distributes foodbank food, clothes, and books at Nipomo Elementary school; hosts weekend nutrition program at local schools
Supporting South SLO County; Arroyo Grande, Grover Beach, Nipomo, Oceano, and Pismo Beach
Email South County Womenade
| Call: 805-270-5751
SLO Food Bank
Provides and connects you with food resources including food distributions, meals, pantry sites, and more
Use the Food Resources Map
to find a location near you
Call: 805-238-4664
Tangelo
Prescription food service delivering healthy meals or groceries to your door; Free for many CenCal clients and other insurance plans
This is an online resource
Call: 254-415-5086
The Traveling Children's Closet & The Teen Closet
Free clothing, school supplies, and resources for newborns-teens
Arroyo Grande, Nipomo, Santa Maria, and San Luis Obispo
Call: 805-709-8673

San Luis Obispo County Bar Association's Lawyer Referral and Information Service
Helps you: find an attorney that matches your needs, determine if you need an attorney or provide alternate resources, and provides you with direction when legal matters are confusing; $50 service fee, does not include attorneys' fees

Superior Court of California, County of SLO Family Law Services

Supports victims and witnesses of crimes by providing support through the court process, victim rights and resources, offender location and release notification, appeals notifications, property return, and more

Court services for parents of minor child(ren) following separation or divorce; Includes: mediation, domestic violence & restraining orders, child custody evaluations, stepparent/domestic partner adoptions, and more

Family Court Mediation Services
California State Law requires mediation in cases when parents are in disagreement about the custody of child(ren); This process is confidential

Call: 805-706-3608

Self-Help Centers

A County library that provides current legal materials, access to legal databases, and a self-help center for all SLO County residents

Superior Court of California Self-Help Center
A free, online resource for self-represented parties in civil cases including: child support, custody, & visitation, divorce, restraining orders, family law, adoptions, and more; Workshops are available; Habla Espanol
This is a virtual resource center
Call: 805-706-3617

A free college savings account for all children born in California since July 1, 2022; You must claim the account for your child

Issues birth and death certificates older than two (2) years
